article_meta.title: US News exclusive ICE agent who opened fire on Renee Good was dragged, hospitalized by illegal migrant driver last year

reasoning: The model produces a mixed result. It correctly identifies some genuine author-prose bias (items 1, 2, and 4) but commits one attribution rule violation (item 3 - 'mob of agitators' is attributed to DHS Secretary Kristi Noem). It also misses some instances of author bias. The bias type labels are mostly reasonable but have some debatable aspects.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Item 3 flags 'mob of agitators' which is clearly attributed to DHS Secretary Kristi Noem ('Noem said...'). This is an attribution rule violation. The other three items are from the author's own prose. One violation out of four items is a moderate error.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Items 1, 2, and 4 are defensible as author-level bias. Item 1 ('serial illegal immigrant...lengthy rap sheet') is the author's own characterization and is arguably sensationalist. Item 2 ('sky-rocketing') is clearly loaded author word choice. Item 4 ('poured gasoline on the long-smoldering tension') is the author's own metaphorical/interpretive framing. Item 3 is a false positive due to attribution violation. So 1 out of 4 items is problematic.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed several defensible instances: 'embattled Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz' (Subjective Qualifying Adjective in author prose), 'a whopping 8,000% surge' (Sensationalism/Word Choice), 'fought back — at times violently — against the presence of agencies like ICE enforcing the law in their communities' (Slant - framing protesters as fighting against law enforcement), and the overall structural slant favoring the ICE/law enforcement perspective throughout.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Item 1 labeled Sensationalism/Emotionalism is reasonable. Item 2 labeled Word Choice is correct. Item 3 is an attribution violation so label accuracy is moot. Item 4 labeled Opinion Statements Presented as Fact is debatable - could also be Word Choice or Sensationalism/Emotionalism, but the label is defensible since it presents an interpretive framing as factual narrative.
